schema.productName: Semi-Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot - HELYX
schema.productDescription: Taypro HELYX is a 39 kg pick-and-place, semi-automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot. Counter-rotating UV-stable PBT brushes remove 95%+ dust in a single pass, clean a 1 MW plant in under 2 hours, run up to 3 km on a single charge, TÜV NORD certified, and built for fixed tilt, seasonal tilt and single-axis tracker plants (see GLYDE-X or NYUMA-X).
schema.howToName: How to clean solar panels with the Taypro HELYX Semi-Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot
schema.howToDescription: Step-by-step deployment of the Taypro HELYX pick-and-place semi-automatic solar panel cleaning robot, from lifting the robot onto the first row through recharging at the docking station.

primaryCta.title: Request a HELYX quote

0.title: Pick-and-Place Deployment
0.description: Lift-and-shift design lets a two-person team deploy HELYX across multiple arrays simultaneously, ideal for scattered, irregular or multi-block solar plants.

1.title: 100% Waterless Single-Pass Cleaning
1.description: Dual counter-rotating PBT brushes lift over 95% of accumulated dust in a single pass, no water, no detergent, no run-off.

2.title: UV-Stable PBT Brush System
2.description: Industrial-grade UV-stable polybutylene terephthalate brushes are engineered for long brush life, stable bristle stiffness and panel-safe contact even under continuous outdoor cleaning cycles.

3.title: Ergonomic 39 kg Chassis
3.description: Light-but-rigid 39 kg construction with integrated fixed handles enables effortless installation, retrieval and inter-row transfer, without any lifting equipment.

4.title: Edge-Detection Safety
4.description: On-board edge-detection prevents accidental falls from the module edge, protecting the robot, the plant and the operator throughout every cleaning cycle.

5.title: Up to 3 km Range Per Charge
5.description: A high-capacity lithium-ion pack delivers up to 3 km of cleaning range on a single charge, enough to clean a 1 MW solar plant in under 2 hours.

6.title: Operator-Centric Onboard Controls
6.description: Simple onboard controls let any site personnel operate HELYX with zero specialised training, eliminating dependency on skilled labour for daily cleaning.

7.title: TÜV NORD Certified Build
7.description: Independently certified by TÜV NORD for IP55 protection, validated under simulated sandstorms (10 g/m²) and tested for damp-heat and dry-heat extremes.

0.title: Counter-Rotating PBT Brush System
0.body: Two counter-rotating UV-stable PBT brushes work in opposing directions to lift loose dust upward while sweeping fine particulates outward, eliminating over 95% of soiling in a single forward pass. The PBT bristles are non-abrasive, panel-safe and engineered for long life under continuous outdoor cleaning.

1.title: Cleans a 1 MW Plant in Under 2 Hours
1.body: A cleaning speed of 10–15 metres per minute means a typical 1 MW utility-scale block is fully cleaned in less than 2 hours, making daily or alternate-day cleaning operationally viable even on large solar farms with constrained labour windows.

2.title: Up to 3 km Range on a Single Charge
2.body: The high-density lithium-ion battery delivers a maximum operational range of up to 3 km per charge, with a recommended range of 1.6 km for sustained daily duty cycles. One unit can comfortably finish several arrays before needing to recharge at the docking station.

3.title: Pick-and-Place Multi-Array Deployment
3.body: Built around a true lift-and-shift workflow, HELYX is moved between arrays by the operator using factory-fitted ergonomic handles. Inter-row traversal is supported by purpose-designed bridge kits supplied and installed by Taypro, letting one robot service multiple rows without dedicated rails or panel modifications.

4.title: Edge-Detection & Fall-Prevention
4.body: Onboard edge-detection sensors continuously scan the module boundary and stop the robot at the panel edge, preventing falls, panel damage and operator risk. A precision-engineered drive system holds the robot stable even at maximum module tilt of 45°.

5.title: Rapid Field Serviceability
5.body: Critical components (brushes, drive wheels, battery, controllers) can be accessed and replaced without dismantling the main assembly. Most field service operations are completed in minutes, not hours, minimising planned and unplanned downtime.

6.title: Reinforced Heavy-Duty Chassis
6.body: The reinforced chassis withstands daily lifting, transport and outdoor exposure across the full 20-year design life. The corrosion-resistance class C3 finish protects against typical industrial atmospheres found on Indian utility-scale plants.

7.title: Sealed Body, IP65 Protection
7.body: All electrical components, wiring harnesses and connectors are fully enclosed within the sealed device body, providing IP65-rated protection against dust, moisture and environmental contaminants in extreme field conditions.

0.name: Lift and place the robot on the first solar row
0.text: Using the factory-fitted ergonomic handles, two operators lift the 39 kg HELYX Solar Panel Cleaning Robot onto the leading edge of the first solar panel row. The compact, balanced chassis is designed for tool-free placement without any cranes or forklifts.

1.name: Activate the onboard controls
1.text: The operator powers on the robot and selects a cleaning cycle from the intuitive onboard controls. No specialised training is required, the interface is designed for any site personnel and starts a single forward cleaning pass along the row.

2.name: Counter-rotating PBT brushes lift dust waterless
2.text: Two counter-rotating UV-stable PBT brushes engage the module surface, lifting and sweeping over 95% of accumulated dust in a single dry pass at a cleaning speed of 10–15 metres per minute, without water, detergent or operator supervision.

3.name: Edge-detection prevents falls at row ends
3.text: Precision edge-detection sensors continuously scan the panel boundary and automatically stop the robot at the end of the row, preventing falls and protecting both the robot and the solar modules.

4.name: Move the robot to the next row via bridge kit or lift-and-shift
4.text: Operators retrieve the robot using the integrated handles and transfer it to the next row using Taypro's purpose-designed bridge kits, or lift-and-shift it directly across short inter-row gaps. The same robot can clean multiple arrays in sequence.

5.name: Recharge and review performance
5.text: After completing the assigned arrays (up to 3 km per charge), the robot is returned to its docking station for lithium-ion recharging. Cleaning telemetry and operational health data are available via the NECTYR portal for fleet-wide review.

0.question: What is a Semi-Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot, and how does Taypro HELYX work?
0.answer: A semi-automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot is a portable robotic cleaner that is manually deployed by an operator onto a row of solar panels, after which it cleans autonomously along that row. Taypro HELYX is a pick-and-place, lift-and-shift type robot: the operator places it on the first row using factory-fitted handles, starts the cleaning cycle via onboard controls, and the robot uses two counter-rotating UV-stable PBT brushes to sweep over 95% of dust off the module surface in a single pass. Once the row is complete, the operator moves the robot to the next row using Taypro's bridge kits or by lift-and-shift.

1.question: How is HELYX different from the GLYDE Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot?
1.answer: GLYDE is a fully autonomous robot that is permanently assigned to a specific array and runs daily cleaning cycles without any human intervention, ideal for large utility-scale plants where labour is the bottleneck. HELYX is a semi-automatic, pick-and-place robot operated by a small two-person team across multiple arrays, ideal for scattered, irregular, smaller or rooftop plants where the capex of a per-array robot isn't justified. Both robots are waterless, AI-driven and managed from the same NECTYR portal.

2.question: How big a solar plant can HELYX clean in a day?
2.answer: A single HELYX can clean approximately 1 MW of solar capacity in under 2 hours, which translates to several MW per shift depending on plant layout, inter-row distance and operator workflow. Most semi-automatic deployments use multiple HELYX units in parallel to cover utility-scale plants on a daily or alternate-day cleaning cycle.

3.question: Does HELYX require water for cleaning solar panels?
3.answer: No. Taypro HELYX is a 100% waterless solar panel cleaning robot. It uses a pair of counter-rotating UV-stable PBT brushes that physically lift and sweep dust off the module surface in a single dry pass, eliminating water consumption, detergent costs, run-off contamination and the logistics of water tankers entirely.

4.question: Is the PBT brush safe for solar modules and Anti-Reflective Coating (ARC)?
4.answer: Yes. The UV-stable PBT brush system is engineered to be panel-safe and non-abrasive. Every robot is independently tested for micro-crack formation, optical reflectance loss and ARC preservation under simulated daily cleaning cycles by accredited solar-sector testing laboratories. HELYX is also TÜV NORD certified for IP55 protection and validated for damp-heat and dry-heat performance.

5.question: What types of solar plants is HELYX suitable for, rooftop, ground-mounted, fixed tilt or tracker?
5.answer: HELYX is built for fixed-tilt, seasonal-tilt and single-axis tracker plants (see GLYDE-X or NYUMA-X). It supports module tilts up to 45° and east–west terrain slopes of up to 15°, making it compatible with most utility-scale, commercial and industrial rooftop, and ground-mounted installations. For dedicated single-axis tracker plants we recommend GLYDE-X; for fully autonomous large utility-scale plants we recommend GLYDE.

6.question: How much does the Taypro HELYX Semi-Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot cost?
6.answer: The price of a HELYX Semi-Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot depends on the number of units, plant layout, bridge-kit requirements and AMC scope. Most customers achieve a fast payback because HELYX replaces recurring water, labour and tanker costs with a single capex investment. Contact our team for a site-specific quote and a detailed ROI calculation.

7.question: How many people are needed to operate a Taypro HELYX robot?
7.answer: Just two. The 39 kg optimised weight with factory-fitted ergonomic handles allows a compact two-person site team to lift, place, operate and relocate HELYX across multiple arrays without any forklifts, cranes or heavy lifting equipment. Operators do not require specialised training, the onboard controls are designed for any site personnel.

8.question: Does HELYX work in extreme Indian climatic conditions like dust storms, heat and monsoon?
8.answer: Yes. HELYX is engineered for harsh Indian utility-scale field conditions: maximum operating temperature of 90°C, IP65-rated sealed enclosure, TÜV NORD-certified IP55 protection and field-validated under 12 simulated sandstorm cycles per year at 10 g/m² sand loading. The robot can clean in winds up to 40 km/hr and locks securely at its docking station up to 180 km/hr.

9.question: What kind of after-sales support does Taypro provide for HELYX?
9.answer: Every HELYX deployment is backed by Taypro's pan-India service model: pre-scheduled preventive and corrective maintenance, immediate remote diagnostics upon incident notification, and same-day on-site breakdown resolution targets, anywhere in India. Annual maintenance contracts (AMC) are available for long-term operations.

howItWorks.title: How Does the HELYX Solar Panel Cleaning Robot Work?
howItWorks.subtitle: HELYX is engineered around a simple, repeatable pick-and-place workflow that any site team can execute. A full cleaning cycle on a 1 MW block typically takes under 2 hours from first lift to dock.

featuresSection.title: Inside the HELYX Semi-Automatic Cleaning Robot
featuresSection.subtitle: Every sub-system on HELYX, from the brush head to the chassis, is engineered for high uptime, fast deployment and long-term panel safety on utility-scale solar power plants.

certifications.title: Certifications & Field-Validated Testing
certifications.subtitle: Taypro's cleaning solutions are subjected to rigorous field and laboratory validation, simulating daily cleaning cycles under realistic outdoor conditions, conducted by accredited solar-sector testing laboratories.
certifications.card0Body: Independently tested and certified by TÜV NORD for IP55 protection and validated under extreme damp-heat and dry-heat performance conditions.
certifications.card1Body: Validated under 12 sandstorm events per year at a sand loading of 10 g/m² per cycle, the toughest desert-climate cleaning protocol.
certifications.card2Body: Tested for micro-crack analysis, full electrical parameter evaluation, optical reflectance and ARC (Anti-Reflective Coating) preservation.

trustStats.title: Trusted by India's Leading Solar Operators

servicePromise.title: Service & Maintenance Promise
servicePromise.subtitle: Every HELYX deployment is backed by a structured service model designed to keep your fleet running at peak uptime across India.
servicePromise.card0Body: All preventive and corrective maintenance activities are executed on a pre-scheduled basis, designed to eliminate unplanned downtime and sustain peak operational performance across the robot's 20-year lifecycle.
servicePromise.card1Body: Taypro provides immediate remote diagnostics and troubleshooting upon incident notification, most issues are resolved without requiring a physical site visit.
servicePromise.card2Body: When on-site work is required, Taypro targets same-day on-site breakdown resolution, available pan-India, with immediate remote diagnostics from NECTYR.

manualVsRobotic.title: Why Choose a Semi-Automatic Cleaning Robot Over Manual Cleaning?
manualVsRobotic.subtitle: Manual cleaning crews, water tankers and ad-hoc schedules can't keep up with daily soiling losses on utility-scale solar plants. Even a small fleet of HELYX robots transforms solar O&M economics overnight.

row0.robot: Daily or alternate-day cycles by a 2-person team

row1.manual: 1.5–3 litres of water per module per wash
row1.robot: Zero, fully waterless single-pass dry cleaning

row2.robot: 95%+ dust removal in a single automated pass, every cycle

row3.manual: Many hours per MW, constrained by daylight and labour
row3.robot: Cleans a 1 MW plant in under 2 hours

row4.manual: Large crews, panel damage risk, night-shift safety risk
row4.robot: 2 operators, ergonomic 39 kg lift, edge-detection safety

row5.manual: Soiling losses of 5–25% accumulate between washes
row5.robot: Frequent cleaning sustains peak PR, lifting plant yield 4–8%

row6.manual: Risk of micro-cracks, ARC abrasion and scratches
row6.robot: Panel-safe UV-stable PBT brushes, lab-tested for ARC preservation

row7.manual: Recurring water, labour, tanker, equipment and downtime costs
row7.robot: One-time capex, replaces recurring opex, 20-year design life

helyxVsGlyde.title: HELYX vs GLYDE: Which Solar Cleaning Robot Is Right for You?
helyxVsGlyde.subtitle: Both robots use waterless dry cleaning and are managed from the same NECTYR. The choice depends on plant size, labour availability and capex appetite.

row0.glyde: Fully autonomous, permanently assigned to array

row1.glyde: Large utility-scale plants with daily cleaning needs
row1.helyx: Multi-block, scattered, smaller or rooftop plants

row3.glyde: 10–15 m/min, runs daily, after production hours
row3.helyx: 10–15 m/min, cleans 1 MW in under 2 hours

row6.helyx: Lower per-MW capex, scales with operator team

indianConditions.title: Built for Indian Solar Plants, From Rooftop to Utility Scale
indianConditions.subtitle: Rajasthan dust storms, Gujarat heat, Karnataka humidity and Tamil Nadu monsoons, Taypro HELYX is engineered, tested and deployed for the realities of large-scale Indian solar O&M.
indianConditions.card0Body: Increased cleaning frequency is the only economical way to neutralise the 8–25% soiling losses typical of Indian utility-scale plants. HELYX's 1 MW-in-2-hours cleaning rate makes daily or alternate-day cycles operationally affordable.
indianConditions.card1Body: Validated for operating temperatures up to 90°C and TÜV NORD certified for damp-heat and dry-heat performance, proven across Indian summer, monsoon and post-monsoon cycles.
indianConditions.card2Body: Many Indian solar farms are in water-stressed districts. HELYX's waterless cleaning removes water tankers from your O&M plan entirely, freeing water and logistics for higher-priority site use.
indianConditions.card3Body: Same-day on-site breakdown resolution across India, with immediate remote diagnostics from NECTYR, backed by regional spare inventory and structured AMC programs.
